(1 comment)

Please add the comments suggested by Andreas. Otherwise I am fine with this.

It's sad that using -Wunused-but-set-variable would have prevented the error introduced by c09b59bb958f3e089b95dd9492cc1bef17cb3782, but the kernel's endless garbage headers won't compile if the flag is set.

(1 comment)

This isn't working correctly for some reason, as it failed to start even
a sinlge test for any test session.  From the MDS/MGS console logs on
https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sets/4666f150-437c-11e6-acf3-5254006e85c2
this appears to be stuck in a loop or similar.

parents.subject: LU-8297 obd: release MGC device if connect fails

commit.subject: LU-8354 ldlm: soft lockup in ldlm_plain_compat_queue
commit.message:
LU-8354 ldlm: soft lockup in ldlm_plain_compat_queue

commit c09b59bb958f3e089b95dd9492cc1bef17cb3782
LU-3963 ldlm: convert to linux list api

breaks loop iteration optimisation.
tmp variable is modified later to skip already
processed locks from the same mode group.

Change-Id: I4dd493d6bd5bb2ac54801cb289dc89efabdfa920
Seagate-bug-id: MRP-3611
Signed-off-by: Andriy Skulysh <andriy.skulysh@seagate.com>
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/21093
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
